name: Kitchen Schedule — Week of 02/16/2026 (Capacity & Financial Snapshot)
subject: Week-of kitchen staffing and capacity snapshot for the week of February 16, 2026. Combines a per-person shift schedule (17 named people + 4 captain slots), a financial bandwidth summary (weekly revenue/cost gap), capacity self-assessment ('enough to staff 3 events AVR maybe 4'), and several 'urgent need' flags on specific staff. A single-week ops document — most valuable as a schema reference for how Comeketo thinks about kitchen ops and as a signal about underlying staffing fragility.
source_files: Schedule of Catering Jobs Weekend   - Kitchen Sched. 02_16_26.csv

financial_bandwidth_snapshot.bandwidth_interpretation: Negative bandwidth — the week was projecting a $4,737.69 shortfall at the time this snapshot was taken. Either actual revenue fell short of projected costs, or a tasting+labor+food+overhead sum exceeded bookings for that week.

actual.projected_event_capacity: enough to staff 3 events AVR maybe 4

financial_bandwidth_snapshot.interpretation: The source sheet poses the questions 'What is the labor allotment?' (Cath/Rod), 'What is the food allotment?' (Bibi), 'Week value?' (Camila) as open questions with the listed dollar figures as the current answer. This is a running weekly-budget discipline, not a one-off. The bandwidth number is the delta between those allotments and the actual bookings for the week.

captains_on_schedule.name: Rodrigo
captains_on_schedule.notes: Owner/founder — listed but no shift times for this week

captains_on_schedule.name: Carlos
captains_on_schedule.note_portuguese: criar uns projetos essa semana ta fraco de eventos
captains_on_schedule.note_english: 'Need to create some projects this week — events are weak.' Self-aware that the week is thin.

captains_on_schedule.name: Juan David
captains_on_schedule.note: Listed but no shifts this week.

crew_on_schedule.name: Cristiane Enaile (amida da Marcilene)
crew_on_schedule.note: 'amida da Marcilene' = 'friend of Marcilene' (Portuguese). Referral hire.

urgent_need_flags.context: Only 6 hours planned but flagged urgent need — probably urgent-need-FOR-Ana, i.e. she needs hours, not that ops urgently needs her. Verify.
Sunday-only shift planned. Urgent need — same ambiguity.

capacity_assessment.self_reported: enough to staff 3 events AVR maybe 4
capacity_assessment.interpretation: Kitchen capacity target for this week is 3 events / possibly 4. This is a useful capacity-planning constant — 3-4 events/week as a rough ceiling for this staffing profile.
capacity_assessment.cross_reference_signal: The Event Labor Projection shows Week 25 peaking at 7 events. That's roughly 2× the capacity assessed here. Either the week-25 load requires dramatically more staff (confirmed by the 'urgent need' flags on current roster) or that week will burn out captains/crew. Flag for summer-staffing planning.

cross_references.staff_roster_overlap: All named people here appear (or should appear) in staff_labor_roster.json. This week's schedule is a delta on that roster — who's working, who's off, who needs hours. Good source to verify roster slugs.
cross_references.captain_pattern: This week shows 6 captain slots — Rodrigo, Bibi, Carlos (semi), Harry, Benny, Juan David. Staff roster knowledge file listed 7 captains. Harry and Benny OFF both Monday and Tuesday suggests a captain rotation — captains are not all scheduled every week.
cross_references.english_portuguese_mix: Portuguese notes present ('criar uns projetos essa semana ta fraco de eventos', 'amida da Marcilene'). Team is bilingual internally. Rodbot voice policy needs to handle this.

feeds_into: knowledge/staff_labor_roster.json — urgent-need flags and per-person planned hours are data that should periodically update the roster's status field.

projects/* — negative bandwidth ($4,737.69 shortfall) is a project trigger. Either (a) weak-events-this-week is normal for February and not worth tracking, or (b) this is part of a multi-week pattern worth a project record. Need multi-week view to tell.
A future 'kitchen ops dashboard' — the structure here (bandwidth / labor allotment / food allotment / overhead / capacity) is the skeleton of a real weekly ops-review.
operational_hooks.gap_to_close: Single-week snapshot. To be useful the team needs a multi-week view — we should get subsequent kitchen-schedule exports and track bandwidth trend, capacity utilization, and urgent-need recurrence.

notes: File name says '02_16_26' — Monday February 16, 2026. The day labels in row 1 are 'Monday / Tuesday / Wednesday / Thursday (Thrusday typo) / Friday / Saturday / Sunday'.
'Camila' named as week-value owner is not currently in the bedrock as a person. Consider adding — might be a manager or part of the ops team.
'Cath' named alongside 'Rod' as labor-allotment owner — possibly Catherine / might be a nickname. Unknown. Flag.

This is a lightweight file relative to the Sales Playbook — but it's the cleanest artifact of how Comeketo thinks about operational bandwidth. Worth re-reading once we have the partnership program and Rodbot identity work further along.

sources.diff: Indexed Kitchen Schedule week of 2026-02-16 — financial bandwidth $-4,737.69, capacity '3 events AVR maybe 4', 17 named people + captains, 4 urgent-need flags, KPI ownership (Camila/Cath-Rod/Bibi). Flagged gap vs Week 25 peak (7 events) in event labor projection.
